message:
suggestions: fix misleading restore-worktree suggestions

The previous SaferAlternative for restore-worktree patterns suggested
using `git restore --staged` which is incorrect:
- `git restore FILE` discards working tree changes (dangerous)
- `git restore --staged FILE` unstages a file (completely different)

Replaced with more accurate suggestions:
- SaferAlternative: Use `git stash` to save changes (retrievable)
- WorkflowFix: Commit changes to preserve in history

Also removed unnecessary #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] from
build_suggestion_registry (only 7 lines, doesn't trigger the lint).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
